Strip_Summary: Bold white text on black: 'You killed someone today. Then you drove home. Your daughter asked what's for dinner.'
First-person POV of GCS interior. Hands on joystick. Blue screens showing aerial village at night. Dust and coffee smell detail.
Aerial surveillance view of sleeping village. Small white structures. Desert terrain. Thermal camera tint. Ten thousand feet below.
GCS operator stickman studying screen. Target's house highlighted on monitor. Pattern data overlay. Intimate knowledge of stranger.
Surveillance screen montage: children leaving for school, breakfast scene, mosque route. All moments documented on screen.
Operator's hand writing in logbook. 'It's your job to know' weight. Thick folder growing on desk.
Bold white text on dark blue: 'And one day it'll be your job to make him stop.'
Bold white text on black: 'You never chose this.'
Stickman gazing upward at dramatic sky. F-16 silhouette cutting through tall clouds. Sun rays breaking through. Dream of flight.
Bold white text stacking on grey: 'A chair. A screen. A trailer that could be anywhere. And a war you commute to.'
Young stickman at Air Force recruitment desk. Eager straight-back posture. Recruiter stickman (faceless) across table. Flag on wall.
F-16 poster on recruitment office wall. Young stickman looking up at poster. Imagining himself in cockpit. Warm aspirational light.
Close shot of young stickman's face. Dot eyes wide. 'Wind. Speed. Freedom.' Words forming around head. Dreamy warm wash.
Young stickman in barracks bunk reading phone screen showing MQ-9 drone image. Confused expression. 'RPA' text on screen.
Barracks night scene. Young stickman looking at laptop showing drone. 'It's a drone.' realization moment. Dim overhead light.
Group of cadets in flight suits. One faceless cadet shaking SUBJECT's hand with sympathy. 'Home for dinner' caption floating.
Group laughing. SUBJECT laughing too but isolated in frame. Sentence echoing forward. Training base exterior background.
Two-panel. Left 'Age 18': cadet uniform at recruit office. Right 'Age 22': flight suit at GCS control station. One year compressed.
Stickman in flight suit standing next to windowless GCS trailer. Never sitting inside what he flies. Desert heat shimmer.
Bold white text on dark: 'Your altitude is always zero.'
Wide shot of Creech AFB. Desert in every direction. Low buildings shimmering in heat. Nevada wasteland. Harsh white sunlight.
Interior of GCS trailer. Two seats facing twelve screens. Joystick and throttle. Office-supply-store chair. AC humming overhead.
Split view: GCS interior on left, satellite view of distant circling aircraft on right. 'Other side of the world' connection.
Operator looking at ground imagery on screen. Can see but can't touch. Glass-barrier metaphor. Blue screen glow on face.
Car in base parking lot. Female stickman (shoulder-length hair, faceless) driving. Small child stickman (hair bow, faceless) waving from back seat. Sunset.
Bold white text on dark: 'You think you understand how this works. You don't.'
GCS trailer. Stickman at screens. Shift rotation board on wall. Clock showing 4AM. Bleary blue screen glow.
Screen never off. Days blurring together. Calendar pages turning. Same trailer. Same chair. Monotone blue.
Reaper drone silhouette circling above foreign terrain on screen. 'Always watching' weight. Persistent surveillance. Cold blue tint.
Screen showing target compound labeled 'JACKPOT'. First long-term target. No real name needed. Intel file starting.
Montage of surveillance: six weeks compressed. Same compound. Same man. Daily routine documented. Pattern building.
Surveillance screen showing target eating breakfast, walking son to school, kneeling in prayer at dawn. Rug visible. Intimate details.
Camera zoom on prayer rug color detail. Small child running circles nearby. HD resolution making intimacy unavoidable.
Operator stickman pausing at screen. Target's child same age as his own. 'Not yet' suppression. Cool blue wash fading edges.
Split monitor view: sleep schedule data, Friday dinner pattern, youngest in front yard. Complete knowledge of a stranger's life.
Target's wife hanging laundry on screen. Domestic routine documented. Surveillance as intimacy violation.
Operator stickman hunched forward. More hours watching target than with own family. Isolation visible. Desaturated grey vignette.
Bold white text on dark: 'He doesn't know you exist. But you know everything about him.'
Manila folder labeled 'Pattern of Life' on desk. Official term. Clinical precision against human content.
Operator writing detailed documentation: habits, contacts, family, walking patterns, Thursday visitors. Thick file growing. Every detail catalogued.
File getting thicker each week. Someone above will decide. Chain of command visualized. Dark institutional corridor.
Bold white text on dark: 'You don't decide. You just build the case.'
Stickman driving suburban road. Elementary school, grocery store, park with children playing. Las Vegas suburb. Normal world.
Park children playing - same age as ones watched on screen today. Cross-world parallel. Desaturated cool tint overlay.
Bold white text on dark: 'The drive home is the worst.'
Kitchen doorway. Wife asking about day. 'Fine.' Single word response. Warm domestic light against cold expression.
Son at homework table asking about dad's job. 'Works with computers.' Not a lie. Not exactly. Warm kitchen light.
Stickman standing in kitchen too long. Looking at nothing. Empty gaze. Warm light fading to cold on his side. Isolation.
Backyard barbecue with neighbor. Faceless neighbor kids in sprinkler. Someone handing beer. Weekend suburban normalcy.
Split: Left 'THIS MORNING' - compound smoke on GCS screen. Right 'THIS EVENING' - beer in hand at BBQ. Same stickman both sides.
Stickman sipping beer. Nodding at neighbor. Did not hear what was said. Thousand-yard stare. BBQ smoke rising.
Fighter pilot stickmen (faceless) in flight suits towering over operator. 'Chair force' stigma. Briefing room background.
Stickman alone while fighter pilots walk away. Never pulled Gs. Never dodged missiles. Different kind of war. Cold corridor.
Operator hunched in chair for twelve hours. Split montage: target's breakfast, prayers, child laughing on screen - then waiting for order to end it all.
Bold white text on dark: 'But that doesn't count.'
Two stickmen in identical flight suits side by side in dark GCS. Three thousand hours together. Never visited each other's homes.
Same two operators looking at same screen. Not talking about what they see. Shared silence. Blue screen glow only light.
Bedroom. Stickman in bathroom doorway. Wife (shoulder-length hair, faceless) brushing teeth. Mouth opens then closes. Unsaid words.
Bold white text on dark: 'I watched a man eat dinner with his family and wrote down what time he finished.'
Military doctor's office. 'Psychological whiplash' on medical chart. Clinical explanation. Stickman in white coat (faceless) across desk.
Stickman talking less. Words visually shrinking. Communication fading. Muted color palette.
Three faces noticing: wife turning, son looking up, operator looking down. All separate. None connecting. Grey isolation motif.
Hands wiping on pants under desk. AC clicking off. Total silence in trailer. Physical tension manifest. Tight jaw, rigid posture.
Stickman at desk. No lunch. Jaw clenched. Not sure when body started betraying stress. Physical symptoms accumulating.
Bold white text on dark: 'Your shoulders are up near your ears. You lower them. They go back up.'
Week seven. JACKPOT sitting in yard on surveillance screen. Wife pouring tea. Warm afternoon light on monitor.
Target's youngest running circles around table on screen. The one who plays in front yard. Child detail visible.
Operator staring at child on screen. The one who looks like his. Watched her grow an inch in six weeks. Cold grey vignette.
Bold red text on black: 'Strike authorized. Cleared hot.' Headset crackle visual.
Child sitting next to father on screen. Operator keying mic. 'Civilians in compound' text on comm overlay. Hesitation visible.
Bold white text on black: 'Three seconds. Acknowledged. Cleared hot. Execute.' Flat response. No room for doubt.
Extreme closeup of operator's thumb moving on trigger. Chest skip. Dot eyes wide. Tense thin-line mouth. Then gone.
First-person POV: Hellfire dropping on screen. Eight-second flight countdown. GCS monitor showing missile trajectory line.
Screen flashes white. Then clears. Moment of impact. Sterile digital representation of destruction.
Yard aftermath on screen. No table. No chairs. No tea. No child running. No one sitting. Absence visible. Rubble where life was.
BDA protocol. HD zoom crosshair on rubble. Operator required to stay and watch. Twenty minutes. Zooming into what's left.
Counting what remains. Target confirmed. Clinical assessment of human destruction. Cold blue monitor light.
Sensor operator (buzz cut, faceless) removing headset. Walking toward trailer door. Daylight shaft from opening door. Silence. Neither speaks.
Suburban road. Sun still up. People watering lawns. Sprinklers. Normal afternoon. Stickman driving through it all. Disconnect.
Car radio playing pop song. Hand reaching to turn it off. Silence preferred. Desert highway. Late afternoon light.
McDonald's drive-through. Happy Meal passing through window. Warm receipt in hand. Golden arches. Everyday normalcy.
Small child stickman (hair bow, faceless) hugging operator's legs at door. She's four. Same age as the one on screen. Amber glow fading to cold.
Bold white text on dark: 'Your wife asks how your day was. Fine.'
Stickman lying in dark bedroom. Eyes open. Ceiling above. Ghost image of yard before explosion superimposed. Translucent haunting.
Empty space. 'Just like that.' transition. Over. Shift complete. Life taken. Compressed into nothing.
Next morning GCS. New callsign on screen. Fresh folder. Machine doesn't stop. 'Load a new file.' Cold blue sterile light.
Same GCS chair. Same screens. Day after day after day. But different now. Routine continuing despite fracture.
Hands shaking on joystick. Strike two, three, seven. Tally marks on desk edge. Stopped counting. Easier that way.
Bar scene. Two stickmen at counter. C-130 buddy (faceless) listening over drinks. Trying to explain. Neon bar light.
Friend's face blank. Not because he isn't trying. Words failing. Gap widening between experience and language.
Bold white text on dark: 'Because there's no version of this that makes sense out loud.'
Bathroom. Bottle visible under sink. After shifts, then before. Progression of self-medication. Dark tile, harsh light.
Wife (shoulder-length hair, faceless) holding bottle. 'Doesn't recognize you anymore.' Doorway distance between them.
Operator stickman alone. No response because no self-recognition either. Bottle on counter. Grey desaturated vignette.
Military doctor's office. White-coat stickman (faceless) behind desk. 'Have you experienced combat?' Clinical question. Cold white room.
Stickman sitting still. Never shot at. Never ran. Never heard a bullet. Three negatives stacking. Medical form on desk.
But killed people. Many. From swivel chair in air-conditioned trailer. Las Vegas skyline through memory overlay. Contradiction visible.
Not combat. Not really. Stickman searching for words. Definition gap visible. Medical terminology inadequate.
Doctor writing on clipboard. Patient can't see what. Pen scratching. Official record being created. Cold clinical desk.
PTSD diagnosis form. 'Non-combat related stress' written on file. Labels inadequate. Clinical language failing reality.
VA waiting room. Wheelchair veteran (faceless, both legs missing) across from operator. Looking at operator's legs. Then face. Unspoken judgment.
Silence between them. Nothing to say. Nothing needed. Understanding and incomprehension in same moment. Empty chairs.
Bold white text on dark: 'You were never in danger. That's the problem. That is the whole problem.'
Two-panel. Left 'Age 28': flight suit at GCS controls. Right 'Age 32': office shirt at IT desk. Identical screen setup. Civilian transition.
IT office desk. Screen. Eight hours. Identical setup to GCS. But no one dies. Mundane corporate environment.
Office scene. Filing cabinet fallen. Coworkers flinching. Loud crash visual. Fluorescent corporate lighting.
Stickman frozen at desk. Different from flinching. Freeze response. Same wiring, different context. Rigid still posture.
Empty house. Wife gone. Took the house. Stickman not fighting. Ring on counter. Sparse lonely furniture. Amber but dim light.
Phone call with daughter. She's eight now. Photo of girl (hair bow) on desk. Want to tell but never do. Night window. Lamp light.
Laptop screen showing Google Maps satellite view. Zooming into foreign village. Searching. Late night blue screen glow.
Scrolling around satellite imagery. Finding a yard with a table. Staring. Same aerial perspective as GCS. Dark room.
Bold white text on dark: 'It's not the same yard. They're all the same yard.'
Near-break moment. Everything blurs - screen, room, hands on trackpad. Then comes back. Sensory overwhelm. Distorted edges.
Stickman blinking. Closing laptop. Darkness returning. Moment passing. Small apartment. Quiet aftermath.
Bold white text on dark: 'You were safe. You were always safe. That is the worst part.'
Another screen. Another desk. Another chair. Echo of every workspace. Simple apartment desk. Evening window light.
Laptop on apartment desk. No one watching but him. No one on other end. Solitary screen. Quiet domestic setting.
No village below. No file getting thicker. Only thing watching is you. Monitor glow in alone apartment. Self-surveillance metaphor.
Closeup of hands on laptop keyboard. Same layout. Same hands. Same chair. Missing one button where trigger was. Quiet composition.
Bold white text on black: 'You killed from ten thousand miles away. And you made it home for dinner. Now you know.'
